description: Direct attribute of one named entity; no relationship traversal (e.g. the chromosome a gene sits on).
Single relationship step out from a seed entity (e.g. genes expressed in an anatomy).
Two chained relationship steps from a seed entity (e.g. genes linked to the diseases a drug treats).
Three or more chained steps; the deepest multi-hop reasoning in the bank.
Count or aggregate over a neighborhood (e.g. how many side effects a compound has).
Entities common to two seeds (e.g. pathways both genes participate in).
Entities of one seed excluding another (e.g. pathways of gene A that gene B lacks).
Ground truth is the empty set; tests refusal to invent a relationship that does not exist.
Yes/no whether a path connects two entities through an intermediate node.
Underspecified description that must be resolved to an entity; answer judged semantically, not by exact match.
